judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are substantively equivalent, correctly identifying the same root cause, fix site, introducing commit, flaw in the earlier reproduction, and flaw in the proposed model-revert plan; Report 2 adds a concrete numeric re-scoring verification (~0.909) and full commit hash, giving it a slight edge in rigor, but neither is meaningfully more complete against the expectations.

report_1.evidence: **Fix**: `config/scoring_config.yaml` — change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label` so it matches the schema the eval set (and `score_batch.py`'s own `REQUIRED_KEYS`) was migrated to.

report_2.evidence: The file that should be changed to fix it is **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`** — its `expected_label_field` value needs to be updated from `label` to `gold_label` so it matches the schema that `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` and `score_batch.py`'s `REQUIRED_KEYS` were migrated to.

report_1.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, exactly matching commit `53551e8` — `chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)` (same date, per `git log`)... The `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` log corroborates this precisely: accuracy hovers around 0.90–0.92 through 2026-06-16, then drops to exactly `0.00` starting `2026-06-17` and stays at `0.00` through every subsequent logged date

report_2.evidence: Commit `53551e8031cfb03aaeda4ba0c8bf22b9aa849cbe`, "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)", is dated `Wed Jun 17 13:00:00 2026 +0000` — same day. Its diff (`git show 53551e8`) renamed the `label` key to `gold_label` in all 11 records of `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` and updated `REQUIRED_KEYS` in `score_batch.py` accordingly, but left `config/scoring_config.yaml` untouched.

report_1.evidence: `score_one.py` (read above) never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never reads `expected_label_field`, and never compares against a gold label at all — it only calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the raw prediction. It is structurally incapable of exercising the bug, because the bug lives entirely in the batch scorer's gold-label lookup, a code path `score_one.py` doesn't touch.

report_2.evidence: the earlier investigator reproduced via `pipeline/score_one.py`, which — as read in step 8 — never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml` or the eval set and never compares a prediction to a gold label at all... There is no code path by which that script could ever reveal a gold-field lookup bug, no matter how many tickets are run through it by hand.

report_1.evidence: Even in a world where `model_id` did select between model versions, reverting it would not touch `expected_label_field`, so `score_batch.py` would still do `record.get("label")` against `gold_label`-keyed records and still produce `accuracy=0.00`. The bug is entirely in the grading harness's field-name lookup, upstream of any prediction.

report_2.evidence: "Switching to the previous model" wouldn't touch the actual defect (`expected_label_field` pointing at a key, `label`, that no longer exists in the eval fixture), so accuracy would stay at `0.00` after the change, likely reinforcing a false narrative that the regression is "deeper" or model-related and burning further investigation time.
